apple_device.current_dirty_diagnostic: Historical 2026-07-13 ABI2 clean-tree single-device proof passed macOS Swift XCTest 2/2 and a physical iPad Pro 11-inch (M4) build/install/launch/private-container-result roundtrip under stable Xcode 26.6 against commit fa2107cc60971527568d84489d39d4758354416e and canonical digest e9ddee1a3f273ed3788fabba34569da81f70fed6a77fcbdbd072b793b121c4c1. The capture froze and rechecked the signed app executable and Rust static library around the device run; raw local evidence is 0700/0600. The current_dirty_diagnostic field name is retained for schema compatibility, while the selected proof records source_tree_dirty=false. The distinct iPad+iPhone aggregate is recorded separately below. This is source-bound local product-execution evidence under an Apple Development profile, not distribution signing, on-device binary attestation, or a Continuity session-protocol test.
apple_device.device_build: Historical clean-tree diagnostic: SwiftPM XCTest 2/2 passed, libq_periapt_ffi_abi2.a built for aarch64-apple-ios, stable Xcode 26.6 produced warning-clean signed builds, strict codesign verification passed, and app/staticlib hashes remained stable across both physical-device runs.
apple_device.device_install_run: The connected physical iPad and distinct physical iPhone each installed and launched the then-current-source binary, emitted one unique nonce-bound QPERIAPT_DEVICE_PASS marker, returned the exact marker from its app-private container, generated a source-bound schema-v2 proof, and passed strict independent validation. Simulator evidence was not accepted, and the schema-v3 aggregate verified distinct device commitments and run identifiers.

failed_attempts.reason: The operator supplied the signing certificate's personal identifier as DEVELOPMENT_TEAM instead of the installed wildcard profile's team identifier. Stable Xcode 26.6 failed closed at provisioning before install or launch. The corrected team/profile input passed without provisioning updates or device registration.

With the same wrong team input, explicitly allowing provisioning updates still failed closed because stable Xcode had no account for that team. No device-registration permission was enabled. The run established that provisioning fallback was not a valid repair; using the already installed correct wildcard profile passed.

The launch log proves a SpringBoard security/profile-trust refusal before application execution. The operator reported that the iPad was concurrently attached to a Wi-Fi network without working connectivity; after correcting network connectivity, an otherwise equivalent exact-source rerun passed. The log does not independently prove that network state was the sole cause.

An operator preflight passed a CoreDevice record identifier rather than the physical hardware UDID. The matrix validator rejected it before any device build, install, launch, or proof emission; the corrected explicit-UDID invocation then passed.

apple_device.ios_staticlib: cargo build -p q-periapt-ffi --release --target aarch64-apple-ios succeeds and the device runner links target/aarch64-apple-ios/release/libq_periapt_ffi_abi2.a directly.
apple_device.macos_native: SwiftPM binding tests run first and pass 2 ABI2 product XCTest cases, including runtime ABI/suite metadata and signed-policy/OS-random workflow checks, through stable Xcode 26.6's xcrun swift.
apple_device.matrix_install_run: Historical schema-v3 ABI2 clean-tree pass at artifact/device-runs/apple-matrix-fa2107c-xcode266-20260713T013800Z/apple-device-matrix-proof.json under stable Xcode 26.6 against commit fa2107cc60971527568d84489d39d4758354416e and canonical digest e9ddee1a3f273ed3788fabba34569da81f70fed6a77fcbdbd072b793b121c4c1; exactly one physical iPad on iPadOS 27.0 beta plus one distinct physical iPhone on iOS 26.5, with distinct run identifiers and device commitments. Both child proofs and the aggregate passed strict independent verification, build logs contained no warning/error, and raw evidence permissions are 0700/0600.
apple_device.provisioning: uses a local Xcode-managed iOS Team Provisioning Profile with team id sha256 prefix dd5cc4f59c0c, expiring 2027-01-17; proof JSON verifies team, bundle id, selected device membership, profile expiration, codesign entitlements, static Rust FFI linkage, and weak AppIntents linkage.

artifact: Q-Periapt — assumption-minimal, cross-substrate binding assurance for PQ/T hybrid KEMs

performance.boundary: The historical clean-tree schema-v4 proof binds commit fa2107cc60971527568d84489d39d4758354416e and canonical source digest e9ddee1a3f273ed3788fabba34569da81f70fed6a77fcbdbd072b793b121c4c1. It emitted all 491,520 schema-v2 raw records for 81,920 paired samples per profile/operation and passed the unchanged 5% block-median CV limit, both preregistered block scales, and every ratio/delta budget without a dirty or uncontrolled override. The proof records controlled AC-power and nominal-thermal observations before build, before run, after run, and after analysis; process-level CPU readiness and the separate WindowServer/MenuBarAgent operational observation are outside its authenticated schema. It remains single-host matched-backend evidence, not device energy evidence, distribution provenance, or parity with production X-Wing/PQ3/Signal implementations.
performance.environment: Darwin 27.0.0 arm64 Apple M1 Max, AC power, nominal thermal state at pre-build, pre-run, post-run, and post-analysis observations

previous_failed_attempts.reason: The prior ABI2 candidate source emitted all 491,520 raw records and passed raw schema validation, but host instability exceeded the fixed CV limit. No proof was emitted and the numeric performance budgets were not evaluated or relaxed.

The current-source 40,960-pair attempt passed raw schema emission but encapsulation primary p99 uncertainty was 1.200220 against the unchanged 1.200000 budget. No proof was emitted. The raw is retained; the follow-up doubled the declared sample count to 81,920 without changing the budget.

The current-source 20,480-pair attempt passed schema, source, environment stability, p50 and p95 budgets, but encapsulation p99 uncertainty exceeded the fixed 1.20 budget at both the primary (1.212481) and 256-pair regression-guard (1.243603) scales. No proof was emitted. The raw is retained; any follow-up uses a larger declared sample count without changing the budget.

The former 256-pair estimate blocks supplied only three nearest-rank p99 tail observations and produced block ratios from 0.24 to 4.28. The failed raw remains diagnostic only and was not reused for proof emission.

continuity_model.boundary: Live source-bound model, strict PrekeySelectionV1 byte correspondence, and modeled-projection diagnostics only. No deployed wire protocol, manifest/prekey/directory authenticity, lease/consumption/tombstone state, projection-completeness proof, spec-to-Rust refinement, production persistence, session-security proof, or ABI2 release attestation.

footprint_bytes.note: Platform-dependent; regenerate per host with `sh paper/footprint.sh`.

provenance.note: proof_source_tree_sha256 records and checks consistency with the canonical execution-input tree; because this manifest is excluded from that digest, it does not bind the manifest root. snapshot_commit names the last source-changing commit for that tree; later results-only evidence successors bind the manifest and selected proofs without changing the canonical source digest. Each nested device/performance proof retains its own source digest and must be rerun after source changes. The proof's explicit source_tree_dirty field distinguishes clean provenance from diagnostics; only clean committed or signed release provenance can bind the manifest root.

purpose: Expected-result manifest so a reviewer can detect data drift. Selected hashes and the canonical execution-input digest are checked before execution. That digest excludes only artifact/results.json and paper/camera-ready-results.txt to avoid self-reference. The transcript and selected proofs are bound by named path/hash fields in this manifest; the manifest root itself must be bound by clean committed or signed release provenance. Formal, package, and device evidence remain separately scoped.

rust_publish.boundary: This section records a clean-tree pre-publication dry-run, not an upload or registry attestation. Any registry publication is independently evidenced by crates.io plus release provenance for the published versions; this section never asserts upload.

swift_xcframework.boundary: This is the current Developer ID-signed Apple-only static XCFramework release candidate for SwiftPM binaryTarget consumption. It is not yet a public or immutable GitHub release and has no remote-consumer claim. The static SDK payload has no standalone executable or notarizable bundle, so SDK notarization and stapling are not applicable; the final consuming macOS app still requires its own signing and notarization, and the final iOS app requires signing and provisioning.
swift_xcframework.current_local_status: Current 2026-07-16 clean-tree signed r1 candidate built from source commit 5664fd86a617f92b620ea37e7692d3417d0e307d with Rust 1.96.1, Cargo 1.96.1, Xcode 26.6, and Swift 6.3.3. All five Apple Rust targets, exact nine-symbol ABI2 exports, schema-3 distribution identity, schema-5 manifest toolchain identity, static-archive path hygiene, strict Developer ID signature, deterministic ZIP, three isolated SwiftPM product tests, macOS universal link/runtime, and iOS device/simulator links passed. The exact four local assets and completion ledger agree. Public tag creation, immutable publication, GitHub attestation, fresh redownload, and remote consumer verification remain pending and are not represented as complete.
swift_xcframework.mode: Developer ID-signed SwiftPM binaryTarget release candidate
